title: Fixed-Length, Close-Delimited, Absent, and Incomplete Content · Language Lens: Bounded Content Length Reader
summary: A body reader allocates from attacker Content-Length, treats premature EOF as success, consumes the next pipelined message, and reuses a connection after incomplete content. Planned artifact: Add and verify the bounded content length reader increment in bounded fixed-length and close-delimited content reader. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Stream exactly the validated content length through independent policy limits without allocating the declared size.
Record framing source, expected bytes, observed bytes, completion, residual buffer, close requirement, and rejection reason.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Predict Bounded Content Length Reader · Theory Bounded Content Length Reader
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory protocol team handles case 5729 in a privacy-sensitive proxy translating between two parser implementations. Build deterministic Go evidence to separate exact-length, incomplete, and response-only close-delimited content; preserve this operating constraint: diagnostics must omit credentials and attacker-controlled metric labels; then reject a protocol transition while optimistic bytes are buffered and prevent desynchronization. Browser code models bounded byte and state decisions only. Sockets, listeners, live TLS, proxies, packet capture, fuzz campaigns, races, load, HTTP/2, HTTP/3, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Bounded Content Length Reader contract for the supplied data.
